description: Lock Charcoal Production to intentional wood carbonization under restricted oxygen, separate from pottery firing and later specialized pit/mound kiln systems.

checks.reason: Controlled fire is the broad predecessor for intentional carbonization.
FAO defines charcoal production by restricted-air wood pyrolysis rather than fired-clay vessels or ceramic kilns.
The date is a conservative source-backed anchor from early smelting contexts requiring a charcoal charge, with uncertainty noted.
The later pit/mound kiln node remains the specialized scaling system downstream of basic charcoal production.
Soap making needs ash or alkaline lye, not the separate fuel/reducing-material technology of charcoal production.
